finds.title: Spec-supplied Zenodo identifiers do not match the actual PCF-1 v1.3 deposit
finds.what_19941678_actually_is: redirects via Zenodo API to record 19972394 (Channel Theory for Polynomial Continued Fractions paper, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19972394) -- a different paper
finds.what_19963298_actually_is: PCF-2 program statement (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19963298, file pcf2_program_statement.pdf, 558153 bytes)
finds.evidence: GET https://zenodo.org/api/records/19941678 / 19963298 / 19937196 against the public Zenodo API; the existing arxiv_pack_pcf1_v1.3/hash_match.json (written 2026-05-02) already records the correct URL https://zenodo.org/records/19937196/files/p12_pcf1_main.pdf
finds.impact_on_byte_equality_verification: none -- the canonical PDF byte-equality (sha256 63420dbf...d9788ff5e, md5 fbf5449b...aef5e0, 392886 bytes, 16 pages) is independently confirmed against record 19937196's file metadata
finds.recommended_action: operator/Claude-side: update the RESUME_AFTER_REBOOT cheat-sheet (and any picture v1.14 strategic-row references) to use the correct PCF-1 v1.3 Zenodo record id 19937196 / DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19937196. The IDs 19941678 + 19963298 likely came from an outdated transcription that conflated PCF-1, Channel Theory, and PCF-2 program-statement deposits.

finds.title: tar.gz SHA is not deterministic across rebuilds (gzip header timestamp)
finds.context: The pcf1_v1.3.tar.gz SHA-256 recorded in manifest.txt + hash_match.json is build-time-dependent (gzip header embeds mtime). Re-running this prompt will produce a tarball with byte-identical contents but a different SHA-256.
finds.recommended_action: rely on the per-file SHA-256 values in manifest.txt for re-verification, not the tarball SHA itself. If a deterministic tarball is required, use 'gzip -n' or build with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH set to a fixed value.
